title: Policy brief on early tracking in education
summary_en: Policy brief on early educational tracking in Hungary. Reviews the international evidence that early sorting widens between-school inequality without raising mean achievement, and applies it to the Hungarian eight-year and six-year gimnázium system. Shows that early-tracking effects compound through the secondary cycle and contribute to the very high inter-school variance in Hungarian PISA scores.
policy_relevance: Direct input for the Hungarian Education Ministry / OH on secondary-school structure. Argues for delaying tracking and for stronger general-education content in the early secondary years. Politically charged in Hungary because it conflicts with the elite-gimnázium system. Caveat: the international evidence has heterogeneity that the Hungarian application doesn't fully reconcile.
